step_by_step_thinking:
Brachycephaly is caused by the premature closure or fusion of certain cranial sutures. The sagittal suture runs along the midline from front to back through the skull, and its premature fusion would lead to brachycephaly characterized by a short, wide head shape.
\boxed{B} Based on the anatomical description and the definition of brachycephaly, the correct answer is B: Sagittal suture. Premature fusion of the sagittal suture restricts the longitudinal expansion of the skull, resulting in a shorter, wider head characteristic of brachycephaly.
